ksh.name: ksh
ksh.description: Use when a developer wants guided end-to-end delivery of a task or feature - orchestrates the SDLC flow with human approval gates. Start here if unsure which step to run.

ksh-spec.name: ksh-spec
ksh-spec.description: Use when starting a task whose requirements are not yet written down - captures WHAT to build as a short written spec before coding.

ksh-plan.name: ksh-plan
ksh-plan.description: Use when a spec exists and the work needs sequencing before coding - turns the approved spec into a step-by-step build plan.

ksh-code.name: ksh-code
ksh-code.description: Use when a plan is approved and code must be written - implements it surgically, following existing patterns.

ksh-test.name: ksh-test
ksh-test.description: Use when verifying behavior after a code change - runs graded checks with a bounded auto-fix loop and test evidence.

ksh-review.name: ksh-review
ksh-review.description: Use when gating a change before merge - reviews the diff for bugs and quality with severity-tagged findings.

ksh-security.name: ksh-security
ksh-security.description: Use when a change touches auth, payments, user input, secrets, or network-exposed code - audits it with STRIDE and OWASP Top 10 checklists.

ksh-doc.name: ksh-doc
ksh-doc.description: Use when capturing the why at the end of a task - records decisions, tradeoffs and implementation notes.

ksh-fix.name: ksh-fix
ksh-fix.description: Use when a specific bug is reported on existing code - reproduces, root-causes and fixes it with test evidence.
